response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with strong web and full-stack credentials, but the role requires deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His only professional C++ experience was a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; the past decade has centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang web platforms. He lacks the required industrial cybersecurity background (IEC 62443, SDL), MQTT/IoT protocol experience, and message-broker-based large-scale C++ systems work, making him a poor fit despite his solid engineering fundamentals.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and desktop
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers) at Dunnhumby
Strong testing culture and mentoring track record (TDD, workshops, code katas)
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience — roughly six months, and over a decade ago
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, or MQTT/IoT protocol background
Recent career is web/full-stack focused, misaligned with networked multi-threaded C++ systems work

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 exposure
High-performance systems and event-driven architecture background
